message: test(store): add metamorphic property tests for advanced LIST ops [frankenredis-0hmd]

files.patch:
@@ -3,7 +3,7 @@
 {"id":"frankenredis-06pn","title":"Add multi-client concurrent access integration test","description":"# Multi-Client Concurrency Test

## What
An integration test that spawns multiple TCP clients connecting to a running FrankenRedis instance simultaneously, executing interleaved read/write operations, and verifying data consistency.

## Why
The TCP e2e tests use a single client. Real Redis deployments have hundreds of concurrent connections. FrankenRedis uses a single-threaded event loop (like Redis) so this should work, but it has never been tested under concurrent load.

## Implementation
1. Add to crates/fr-server/tests/concurrent_e2e.rs
2. Start frankenredis on random port (release build for realistic performance)
3. Spawn 10 threads, each:
   - Opens TCP connection
   - Sends AUTH if needed
   - Runs 100 SET commands with unique keys (client_{thread_id}_key_{i})
   - Runs 100 GET commands verifying values match what was SET
   - Runs INCR on a shared counter key "global_counter"
   - Runs LPUSH on a shared list key "global_list"
4. After all threads join:
   - Verify shared counter = 10 * 100 = 1000
   - Verify global_list length = 10 * 100 = 1000
   - Verify each client key exists with correct value
   - Verify DBSIZE = expected count (1000 unique client keys + 2 shared keys)
5. Test with pipeline depth: repeat above but batch 10 commands per pipeline send

## Logging
- Each thread logs: "[T{id}] SET client_{id}_key_{i} = value_{i} → OK"
- Each thread logs: "[T{id}] GET client_{id}_key_{i} → PASS/FAIL"
- After join: "global_counter = 1000 (expected 1000) → PASS"
- After join: "DBSIZE = 1002 (expected 1002) → PASS"
- Summary: "10 threads, 100 ops each, 0 failures"

## Files
- crates/fr-server/tests/concurrent_e2e.rs (new)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:21.013893582Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T19:02:21.116591297Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T19:02:21.116317779Z","close_reason":"Implemented concurrent TCP integration tests in existing tcp_e2e_test.rs and validated via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-07w","title":"Upgrade DUMP/RESTORE to use CRC64 and fix bounds checks","description":"## Problem 1: CRC16 instead of CRC64

fr-store/src/lib.rs line 7580: DUMP payload uses CRC16 (2 bytes). Redis uses CRC64 (8 bytes). This means:
- DUMP payloads from FrankenRedis cannot be loaded by Redis (wrong checksum format/size)
- RESTORE payloads from Redis cannot be loaded by FrankenRedis
- Only 16 bits of protection — trivially forgeable (65536 attempts average)

Note: fr-persist already has a CRC64 implementation (CRC64_REDIS_POLY at line 164) for RDB files. We should reuse it.

## Problem 2: Inconsistent bounds checks

fr-store/src/lib.rs: The String type correctly bounds-checks against payload.len()-11 (excluding trailer). List/Set/Hash/SortedSet check against payload.len() (including trailer). This allows the parser to read trailer bytes as data in crafted payloads.

## Implementation Plan

1. Import/expose the CRC64 function from fr-persist (or implement a shared crc64 utility)
2. Change dump_key to write 8-byte CRC64 instead of 2-byte CRC16
3. Change restore_key to validate 8-byte CRC64
4. Update the payload trailer format: [8-byte TTL][1-byte version][8-byte CRC64] = 17 bytes
5. Fix all bounds checks to exclude the trailer length consistently
6. Update DUMP/RESTORE version byte to indicate the new format

## Interoperability Note

After this change, DUMP/RESTORE payloads will use CRC64 like Redis but our internal payload format (type tags, encoding) still differs from Redis. Full Redis DUMP interop would require matching Redis's exact encoding, which is a separate larger task.

## Files to Modify

- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs: dump_key, restore_key, all bounds checks
- crates/fr-persist/src/lib.rs: May need to expose crc64 function as pub","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:09:46.515174128Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:29:11.523094869Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:29:11.522613116Z","close_reason":"DUMP/RESTORE upgraded from CRC16 to CRC64 (using fr-persist::crc64_redis). All bounds checks now exclude 17-byte trailer consistently.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-07w","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-7o5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:09:50.045866469Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
